significant_events.source_quote: The Humane Animal Society (HAS) has been honored with the World Rabies Day Award in the 'Animal Welfare Champion' category by the Global Alliance for Rabies Control (GARC) and Boehringer Ingelheim. This accolade recognizes HAS's 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control. The award includes a cash prize and advanced tools to enhance rabies management capabilities, including the Rabies Vaccination Tracker (RVT), Vaccination and Sterilisation (VST) Tool, Rabies Case Surveillance (RCS) Tool, and Bite Case Tracker (BCT) Tool. This award was given on the One Health Day. It is an international campaign celebrating the need for a One Health approach to address shared health threats at the human-animal-environment interface.
significant_events.summary: HAS received the 'Animal Welfare Champion' award for 17 years of outstanding efforts in rabies prevention and control, including a cash prize and tools.

significant_events.source_quote: From September 22nd to 28th, we conducted a week-long Anti-Rabies Vaccination Drive in Coimbatore for World Rabies Day. Our goal was to vaccinate as many animals as possible and raise public awareness about the importance of vaccinating community pets. Despite the scorching sun, our dedicated team vaccinated 927 animals.
significant_events.summary: A week-long drive in Coimbatore vaccinated 927 animals and raised public awareness about the importance of vaccinating community pets.

significant_events.source_quote: We are delighted to report the successful progress of the Bosch CSR project at the HAS Animal Birth Control Centre. The kennel renovation and kitchen revamp, sponsored by Bosch, were celebrated with esteemed guests Naved Narayan, Centre Head - Coimbatore, BGSW, and Julius Panakkal, Section Head, BGSW. Certificates were awarded to the first batch of employees completing the Animal Sensitization program, promoting compassion through training and education.
significant_events.summary: Multiple batches trained Bosch employees and volunteers in first aid, rescue techniques, wound management, and specialized care for street animals.

significant_events.event_name: Legal Awareness Session for Coimbatore Police
significant_events.source_quote: HAS has led the fight against animal cruelty in Coimbatore, facing challenges due to lack of legal awareness. To address this, we conducted a legal awareness session for rural police officers, supported by Coimbatore Superintendent of Police, Mr. V. Badrinarayanan (I.P.S). Dr. Mini Vasudevan explained the PCA Act and animal welfare laws. Educating law enforcement is crucial for prompt action against cruelty.
significant_events.summary: Conducted a legal awareness session for rural police officers on the PCA Act and animal welfare laws to promote prompt action against cruelty.

significant_events.source_quote: We conducted sessions on February 7th for primary and high school students of Yuvabharathi Public School. The interactive talks sparked active interest, and the school's fundraising activities raised substantial donations for our ABC and Rescue facility. Similarly, at Annai Velankanni Matriculation Higher Secondary School, we engaged around 550 students from grades 4 to 9 in discussions on animal welfare, inspiring thoughtful questions and compassion. We're grateful for these schools' efforts.
significant_events.summary: Conducted interactive sessions for primary and high school students to inspire compassion and raise donations for the ABC and Rescue facility.

significant_events.source_quote: Pawga and the Humane Animal Society (HAS) united to host a joyful event where yoga enthusiasts and indie puppies came together. The gathering promoted yoga benefits and indie dog adoption, with playful pups spreading joy. We thank Pawga for their support and revenue contribution to our rescue animals.
significant_events.summary: Multiple events blending yoga with rescue puppies to promote adoption, raise awareness, and support welfare activities through event proceeds.

significant_events.source_quote: MARS, the company behind Pedigree and Royal Canin, partnered with our ABC and Rescue Center to promote indie dog adoptions. Their team provided essential training to our staff, focusing on adoption promotion and healthy diets for dogs. This initiative recognizes and supports the efforts of animal welfare organizations and promises to transform the lives of countless street animals.
significant_events.summary: Partnership with MARS to promote indie dog adoptions and provide staff training on adoption promotion and healthy diets for dogs.

data.original_text: We have connected more than a million pets and their families with the resources they need.

We believe pets are safest in a humane community that values and protects them, best cared for by a responsible and loving family, healthiest when seen regularly by a veterinarian, and that saving animal lives involves much more than increasing adoptions and decreasing euthanasia. That is why we have many programs and services such as counseling to help people find solutions to keep their pet, offer spay/neuter services to families in ...

One of the most effective ways to reduce euthanasia in our community is to reduce the number of homeless pets. We help keep pets and their people together by providing one-on-one counseling prior to relinquishment and offering our donor-subsidized, low-cost spay/neuter program.

By offering these much-needed services, we help save animal lives by preventing unwanted litters and work towards reducing Wichita's pet overpopulation problem. In 2023 KHS performed 8,136 spay/neuter surgeries. Of those, 1,762 were for low-income pet owners in our community.
We are committed to the prevention of abuse and neglect of animals through adoption, veterinary care, rescue & rehabilitation.

Founded in 1904, the Humane Society of New York is a nonprofit veterinary hospital and no-kill shelter dedicated to making high-quality, affordable veterinary care accessible to those who need it most.
Our fight for stronger animal welfare legislation at the city, state, and federal levels is creating a safer and more humane future, and your actions can help us continue to make a difference!
We make the world better for all animals.
Protecting over 1 billion lives annually through rescue, care, humane standards and conservation efforts.
We go where animals need us most and where few other organizations can, responding to cruelty and disaster situations around the world.
Our sanctuaries give a lifelong home to animals in need, and our rehabilitation centers have helped heal thousands of animals on their way to loving homes.

We build bridges and allies. Through grants, training, partnerships and events, we help shelters; rescue organizations; veterinarians; law enforcement officers; local, state and federal governments; community leaders; and the public change animals' lives.
We take on cruel practices. From factory farms to puppy mills, we target the most deeply rooted animal abuses affecting the greatest number of animals around the world.
Our undercover investigations, awareness campaigns and advocacy move the public, corporations and legislators to act for animals.
Caring for animals and empowering communities.
HARP operates two low-cost, high-quality veterinary medical centers, treating over 6,500 animals and performing more than 7,000 spay and neuter surgeries annually.

Beyond caring for domestic animals, HARP remains committed to indigenous species through its fully licensed Wildlife Rehabilitation Center, which treats over 4,000 sick, injured, and orphaned wildlife each year, including more than 2,300 mammals, 1,700 birds, and 50 reptiles.
The Houston Humane Society is dedicated to ending animal cruelty, abuse & the overpopulation of animals in Houston, Sugar Land, Houston Metro Area.
The main goal of our Animal Wellness Clinic is to provide affordable and accessible veterinary services to pet owners in the greater Brazoria, Ft. Bend, Harris, and surrounding counties.
Lollypop Farm, the Humane Society of Greater Rochester, provides shelter for homeless or abused animals, and programs to help pets in our community.
Our Mission is to protect and improve the lives of dogs and cats by providing shelter, care, adoption, rescue, spay/neuter programs, and community education.
We provide meaningful, humane direction through an ever-expanding lens of scientific knowledge and grassroots compassion.
We make animals our top priority when it comes to their safety, their treatment, their wellbeing and their place in our world.
1+ billion. Farm animals protected under our welfare standards.
85,000+ Animals fed, sheltered and rescued.
500,000. Animals protected at zoos and aquariums.
1.1 million. animals. have been saved each year through our work promoting plant-based eating.
2,700+ dogs. have been rescued during more than a decade of our work against the now-banned dog meat trade in South Korea.
480+ localities. have passed measures that ban the sale of puppy mill dogs in pet stores.
Since 1954, we have helped pass thousands of local and national laws protecting animals.
We have connected more than a million pets and their families with the resources they need.

And we have taught thousands of professionals how to lead effective investigations, resolve human-animal conflicts with kindness, implement high-volume, high-quality spay/neuter techniques, help street animals and pets living with their families in poverty and more.
Our save rate is now over 90% for cats and dogs, a high standard in the animal welfare community.
Thousands of dogs and cats in San Antonio and its surrounding areas are saved from euthanasia every year due to the mission and practices of the SAHS.
More than 7,000 spay and neuter surgeries annually.
The Wildlife Rehabilitation Center, which treats over 4,000 sick, injured, and orphaned wildlife each year, including more than 2,300 mammals, 1,700 birds, and 50 reptiles.
From January 1 to December 31 of 2023, KHS admitted 10,350 animals to our facility and adopted or found alternative placement for 10,037 animals with an overall Save Rate of 97%.
In 2023 KHS performed 8,136 spay/neuter surgeries. Of those, 1,762 were for low-income pet owners in our community.
In 2023, KHS transferred 2,823 animals to outside groups.
